summary: MAUI app with LiveCharts2 using SkiaSharp 3.119.1 crashes on certain Windows 11 systems with COMException 'Element not found' during WinRT activation of SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.PropertySetExtensions in the ANGLE/OpenGL rendering path.

bugSignals.errorMessage: System.Runtime.InteropServices.COMException: Element not found. → System.TypeInitializationException: The type initializer for 'SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.PropertySetExtensions' threw an exception.
bugSignals.stackTrace: at Marshal.ThrowExceptionForHR → BaseActivationFactory() → PropertySetExtensions() → PropertySetExtensions.AddSingle() → GlesContext.CreateSurface() → AngleSwapChainPanel.EnsureRenderSurface() → AngleSwapChainPanel.OnLoaded()

stepsToReproduce: Create a MAUI app with LiveCharts2 using SkiaSharp 3.119.1 for chart rendering on Windows
Deploy to certain Windows 11 systems (e.g. Lenovo 83C4 with build 10.0.26100.7462)
App crashes on load when the chart view tries to initialize the ANGLE/OpenGL surface
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Windows 11 (10.0.26100.7462), Lenovo 83C4, SkiaSharp 3.119.1, LiveCharts2 (uses SKSwapChainPanel for GPU rendering)

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: PropertySetExtensions is a C++/WinRT component that hasn't changed structurally. The activation failure is environment-dependent, not version-dependent.

analysis.summary: PropertySetExtensions is a C++/WinRT static runtime class defined in SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.dll that provides helper methods for adding Single and Size values to a PropertySet. It's used exclusively in the ANGLE/OpenGL surface creation path (GlesContext.CreateSurface). The COMException 'Element not found' occurs during WinRT class activation, meaning the runtime cannot locate the activation factory for this class. This is an environment-specific deployment issue where the WinUI native component DLL is either not deployed or its WinRT registration is inaccessible.
analysis.rationale: Classified as type/bug because the app crashes with an unhandled exception during a normal rendering path. Area is area/SkiaSharp.Views (not Maui-specific) because the crash is in the WinUI view layer (AngleSwapChainPanel/GlesContext), not MAUI handlers. The 'certain systems' qualifier and the parallel with #2948 (resolved by environment fix) suggest a WinRT activation/deployment issue rather than a code defect in SkiaSharp itself.

keySignals.interpretation: WinRT activation factory cannot find the PropertySetExtensions class — either the native DLL is missing or COM registration is broken.
Environment-specific failure — not all Windows 11 systems affected, suggesting a deployment or registration issue.

keySignals.text: We have tried both with HA enabled and disabled but the result is the same.
keySignals.interpretation: Hardware Acceleration toggle doesn't affect this because the crash occurs before any rendering — during WinRT class initialization.

keySignals.text: This seemed to be now fixed by Microsoft Store adjusting their setup.
keySignals.interpretation: Nearly identical issue #2948 was resolved by an environment change, not a code fix — confirms this is an activation/deployment problem.

codeInvestigation.finding: PropertySetExtensions is a static WinRT runtimeclass with AddSingle and AddSize methods. As a WinRT component, it requires proper activation factory registration at runtime.
Implementation simply wraps PropertySet.Insert with PropertyValue::CreateSingle/CreateSize. The failure is in the WinRT activation infrastructure, not in the method logic.

CreateSurface calls PropertySetExtensions.AddSingle (line 104) and AddSize (line 98) to configure EGL surface properties. This is the crash site — triggered from AngleSwapChainPanel.EnsureRenderSurface when the ANGLE OpenGL surface is created.
OnLoaded creates GlesContext and calls EnsureRenderSurface. This is the entry point for the crash — happens when the SwapChainPanel is first loaded into the visual tree.
SKXamlCanvas uses WriteableBitmap (CPU raster) rendering and does NOT use ANGLE, PropertySetExtensions, or GlesContext at all — completely unaffected by this issue.
The CsWinRT projection includes SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native namespace and uses CsWinRT 2.0.4. CsWinRT generates the managed interop code that calls BaseActivationFactory.

nextQuestions: Is SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.dll present in the deployment output directory?
Is the app deployed as packaged (MSIX) or unpackaged? WinRT activation differs between modes.
Does the issue occur with a clean/new MAUI project using SKSwapChainPanel (without LiveCharts2)?
What is the deployment method (sideload, MS Store, ClickOnce, xcopy)?

resolution.hypothesis: The WinRT activation factory for PropertySetExtensions cannot be found on certain systems, likely due to SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.dll not being properly deployed or registered. This affects only the ANGLE/OpenGL rendering path (SKSwapChainPanel/SKGLView).

proposals.title: Use SKCanvasView instead of SKGLView
proposals.description: If LiveCharts2 supports configuration of the rendering backend, switch from GPU-accelerated (SKSwapChainPanel/SKGLView) to CPU raster (SKXamlCanvas/SKCanvasView) on Windows. SKXamlCanvas uses WriteableBitmap rendering and does not depend on the WinUI native component at all.

proposals.title: Verify SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.dll deployment
proposals.description: Check that SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.dll is present in the published output directory. If missing, the WinRT activation factory will fail with COMException. Ensure the app project has a direct PackageReference to SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI (not just transitively via LiveCharts2).

proposals.title: Add try-catch around ANGLE surface creation with fallback
proposals.description: Wrap GlesContext.CreateSurface or AngleSwapChainPanel.EnsureRenderSurface in a try-catch for TypeInitializationException/COMException and fall back to raster rendering. This would make the failure graceful rather than a crash.

resolution.recommendedReason: The 'certain systems' qualifier and the parallel with #2948 strongly suggest a deployment/registration issue. Confirming whether the native DLL is present will immediately narrow the root cause.

actionability.reason: Need to determine deployment method and verify native DLL presence before diagnosing further. The crash is environment-specific and likely deployment-related based on #2948 precedent.

missingInfo: Is SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.dll present in the published app output?
Is the app deployed as packaged (MSIX) or unpackaged?
What deployment method is used (sideload, Store, ClickOnce)?
Does a minimal MAUI app with SKSwapChainPanel (without LiveCharts2) also crash on the affected system?

actions.description: Update area label to area/SkiaSharp.Views and add OpenGL backend label
Request deployment details and suggest verification steps
actions.comment:
Thanks for the detailed stack trace.

This crash occurs during WinRT class activation of `PropertySetExtensions`, a native component used exclusively in the ANGLE/OpenGL rendering path (`SKSwapChainPanel` / `SKGLView`). We've seen a very similar issue in #2948, which turned out to be environment/deployment-related.

A few questions that will help us diagnose this:

1. **Is the app deployed as packaged (MSIX) or unpackaged?** WinRT activation behaves differently between the two modes.
2. **What deployment method are you using?** (sideload, MS Store, ClickOnce, xcopy)
3. **Could you check whether `SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.dll` is present in your published output directory?** If it's missing, that would explain the activation failure.
4. **Does a minimal MAUI project with `SKSwapChainPanel` (without LiveCharts2) also crash on the affected system?** This would help isolate whether it's a SkiaSharp packaging issue or something specific to how LiveCharts2 references SkiaSharp.

In the meantime, if LiveCharts2 supports switching to CPU-based rendering (`SKCanvasView` instead of `SKGLView`), that would bypass the ANGLE path entirely and should work on all systems.

actions.description: Cross-reference with #2948 (identical stack trace, resolved by environment fix)
